channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Philippine Vice President Duterte asks Supreme Court to void her impeachment and block Senate trial
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Philippine Vice President Sara Duterte asked the Supreme Court on Tuesday to void her impeachment and block a Senate trial that could remove her from office. The House of Representatives, which is dominated by the allies of President Ferdinand Marcos Jr.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: North Korea slams US and Asian rivals for pursuing 'absurd' plans to denuclearize North
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea on Tuesday criticized the United States and Asian neighbors for pursuing the “absurd” goal of denuclearizing the North and said it will push to expand its nuclear forces under the direction of its authoritarian leader Kim Jong Un.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Firefighters battle massive blaze at Pennsylvania business
item.description: A massive fire broke out at a building in Abington Township, just outside of Philadelphia, Monday night.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Police arrest alleged leader of anarchist vegan cult tied to series of crimes, including CBP agent murder
item.description: An alleged cult leader and member wanted in connection to the murder of a Vermont Border Patrol agent were arrested on unrelated charges over the weekend, Maryland State Police confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top Russian and American officials will hold talks on ending the Ukraine war without Kyiv
item.description: RIYADH, Saudi Arabia (AP) — Senior American and Russian officials, including the countries’ top diplomats, will hold talks on improving their ties and negotiating an end to the war in Ukraine, officials said Monday, in what would be the most significant meeting between the sides since Moscow's full-
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, JUSTIN SPIKE and BARRY HATTON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: A look at recent aviation disasters in North America
item.description: The crash of a Delta Air Lines plane in Toronto is the fourth major aviation disaster in North America in the last month.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Convicted NYC drug dealer who ran Times Square operation out of prison after Biden commutation
item.description: A man convicted for his role in a 24/7 drug dealing operation in Times Square is out of prison after his sentence was commuted by former President Joe Biden.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gov. Ron DeSantis' former chief of staff James Uthmeier sworn in as Florida’s attorney general
item.description: Gov. Ron DeSantis on Monday named his former chief of staff, James Uthmeier as Florida’s attorney general, handing the state’s top law enforcement role to a trusted aide who has helped orchestrate and defend some of his most divisive initiatives.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANY MATAT and KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Toronto plane crash timeline: Delta flight from Minneapolis flips upside down while attempting to land
item.description: A Delta Air Lines flight from Minneapolis crashed and overturned while landing at Toronto Pearson Airport on Monday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The moon will change colors next month: Here's why
item.description: People gazing up at the night sky in a few weeks will have the opportunity to see the moon change color during a special celestial event. The total lunar eclipse will appear for one night, starting late on March 13, according to NASA.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Self, KTLA 5

item.title: Judge to rule swiftly on effort to block DOGE from assessing data and firing federal employees
item.description: A federal judge says she will quickly rule on a request to block Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency from accessing sensitive data and firing employees at several federal agencies.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 8 injured after Delta plane flipped over during landing incident in Canada
item.description: A Delta Air Lines plane has flipped upon arrival at Toronto’s Pearson Airport and ended up on its roof, injuring 18 people.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: SpaceX employees visiting FAA amid reported firings
item.description: Members of the SpaceX team will "envision how we can make a new, better, modern and safer system," Sean Duffy said.
item.dc:creator: Steven Joachim, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: More adults are wondering if they have autism: Tips to seek a diagnosis
item.description: More adults have sought insight on their own neurodiversity in the last decade — often after their children are diagnosed or after seeing social media posts.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump suggests no laws violated if he ‘saves’ country
item.description: Trump doubled down on the Napoleon reference on Sunday, reposting an image of the French emperor riding into battle with the text "He who saves his country violates no law" overlaid.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, ABC 4

item.title: NAACP spending guide urges Black Americans to avoid companies that dumped DEI
item.description: The nation’s oldest civil rights organization released its Black Consumer Advisory, a project intended to help educate Black Americans on “who’s pushing progress and who’s stuck in the past.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, KRON4

item.title: Five pressing questions as RFK Jr. takes HHS reins
item.description: His entire Make America Healthy Again movement is based largely on undermining the scientific and public health messaging from the federal government, along with overhauling the status quo in the food and drug industries.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, Fox 5

item.title: Washington Post backs out of 'Fire Elon Musk' ad order
item.description: The Washington Post this week backed out of a “Fire Elon Musk” advertising order that was to run as a wrap on some of its Tuesday editions, according to the advocacy group Common Cause.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KRON4

item.title: Trump administration tries to bring back fired nuclear weapons workers, reversing DOGE cuts
item.description: he Trump administration has halted the firings of hundreds of federal employees who were tasked with working on the nation's nuclear weapons programs, in an about-face that has left workers confused and experts cautioning that DOGE's blind cost cutting will put communities at risk.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Florida sheriff says ICE partnership only the beginning in illegal migrant crackdown
item.description: St. Johns, Florida, Sheriff Robert Hardwick shared how law enforcement is working with federal immigration authorities to apprehend and enforce immigration laws.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: At least 9 people are dead, including 8 in Kentucky, in latest blast of winter weather
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — At least nine people have died in the most recent round of harsh weather to pummel the U.S., including eight people in Kentucky who died as creeks swelled from heavy rain and water covered roads. Kentucky Gov.
item.dc:creator: DYLAN LOVAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Wife convicted of murdering Army soldier husband at home on military base
item.description: A federal jury convicted a Kansas woman for shooting and killing her Army soldier husband in February 2024 at their home at Fort Riley.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mother arrested after leaving 3 children to live alone for years
item.description: Oakland County Sheriff’s Office deputies arrested a 34-year-old Pontiac mother on Friday after she abandoned her three children for years, leaving them in horrendous living conditions.
item.dc:creator: Trevor King, WFLA, NBC

item.title: McDonald's teases 'new' chicken item, return of Snack Wraps in 2025
item.description: We knew Snack Wraps were returning to McDonald's this year, but now it looks like another item we've seen before is coming back.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Transgender man found dead in New York field endured repeated acts torture: police
item.description: “This is one of the most horrific crimes I've ever investigated,” Captain Kelly Swift of the New York State Police Bureau of Criminal Investigation said.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, ABC 4

item.title: Dismantling of federal efforts to monitor election interference creates opening for foreign meddling
item.description: “Our adversaries are upping their game every day,” said former Department of Homeland Security cyber chief Suzanne Spaulding. “I’m worried that we are, at the same time, tearing down our defenses.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Ukraine says US presented doc to access minerals, offered almost nothing in return
item.description: “I didn’t let the ministers sign a relevant agreement because in my view it is not ready to protect us, our interest,” Zelenskyy told The Associated Press on Saturday in Munich.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Suspect in Tupac Shakur killing seeks to delay trial as defense identifies new witnesses
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Attorneys for the man suspected of killing rap icon Tupac Shakur want to delay next month’s trial, saying more time is needed for investigative work to ensure that Duane “Keffe D” Davis gets a fair trial.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Democrats nervously wait on Trump response to court rulings
item.description: House Democrats howling over the flood of unilateral actions streaming from the White House are voicing confidence that the courts will provide a check on potential abuses of power. They’re less certain, however, about how President Trump will respond.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, WGN 9

item.title: Sheryl Crow sells Tesla to donate funds to embattled NPR
item.description: “Money donated to NPR, which is under threat by President Musk, in hopes that the truth will continue to find its way to those willing to know the truth,” the country artist wrote.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, WGN 9

item.title: Justice Department fires 20 immigration judges from backlogged courts amid major government cuts
item.description: The Trump administration fired 20 immigration judges without explanation, a union official said Saturday amid sweeping moves to shrink the size of the federal government.
item.dc:creator: ELLIOT SPAGAT, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Andrew Lester pleads guilty in shooting of unarmed Kansas City teen Ralph Yarl
item.description: Andrew Lester pleaded guilty to second-degree assault after shooting 16-year-old Ralph Yarl outside Kansas City, Missouri home in April 2023
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, Fox 5

item.title: Black Hawk pilots may have missed air traffic directive, flown on 'bad' altitude data before crash
item.description: It is likely the Black Hawk pilots “stepped on” the communication for less than a second by pressing the microphone key to communicate back to the control tower, she said.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, Fox 5

item.title: Homeowner pleads guilty to shooting Black teen Ralph Yarl who rang the wrong doorbell
item.description: LIBERTY, Mo. (AP) — An 86-year-old Kansas City man pleaded guilty Friday to a lesser charge in the 2023 shooting of Ralph Yarl, a Black honor student who rang the white man’s doorbell by mistake, in a case that shocked the country and renewed national debate about gun policies and race in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Justice Department moves to dismiss Eric Adams case
item.description: The Department of Justice (DOJ) has formally asked a federal district court to drop its corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams (D) after federal prosecutors in Manhattan resigned rather than dismiss the case.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Idle buses, empty Border Patrol boats: Arrests for illegal crossings fall in the Rio Grande Valley
item.description: MISSION, Texas (AP) — An idled Border Patrol bus sat empty this week, on standby for any migrants surrendering near the southern tip of Texas. Agents in two speedboats zipped past pockets of sandy shores, known landing spots for people entering the U.S.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: White House limits AP access to Oval Office, Air Force One indefinitely
item.description: The White House says it will limit Associated Press journalists' access to the Oval Office and Air Force One, an escalation of a brooding conflict between the Trump administration and the wire service this week.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5
